response:
# Real-Time Notification Panel with Priority Tiers — Accessibility Design Plan > **For Claude:** Use a11y-planner protocol. Review with a11y-critic after implementation. > **Compliance target:** WCAG 2.2 AA > **Users who need accessibility:** Screen reader users (NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver), keyboard-only users, low-vision users, clinicians under time pressure / high cognitive load > **Assistive technologies:** NVDA (Windows + Firefox/Chrome), JAWS (Windows + Chrome), VoiceOver (macOS + Safari), keyboard-only **Feature:** A real-time, WebSocket-driven notification panel in a hospital EHR with three priority tiers (Critical/Warning/Info), an unread badge, dismissible notifications, a history drawer, and configurable auto-dismiss timeouts. **Risk Level:** HIGH — safety-critical. A missed or mis-announced Critical notification in an EHR is a patient-safety failure, not a UX inconvenience. The current implementation has at least five accessibility defects that directly threaten patient safety and keyboard access. **Component Type:** Composite — Live region announcer system + Disclosure (panel) + Dialog/drawer (history) + Button widgets (bell, dismiss) + Timing control. --- ## Scope & Context ### What is being built? An accessible real-time notification system that announces three priority tiers of clinical notifications to assistive technology with the *correct* interruption behavior per tier, keeps a keyboard- and screen-reader-navigable history, and gives AT users enough time to read auto-dismissing notifications. ### What user need does it address? Clinicians — including those who use screen readers, keyboard-only navigation, or screen magnification — must reliably receive time-critical clinical alerts (critical lab values, pending countersignatures) while not being constantly interrupted by low-priority noise (appointment reminders). The interruption model must match clinical urgency: a critical potassium of 6.8 mEq/L must interrupt; a team-huddle reminder must not. ### Who needs accessibility? - **Screen reader users (primary risk surface):** The entire value of this feature for them is *announcement*. The current single `role="alert"` design both over-interrupts (Info/Warning barge in) and is unreliable (DOM-append race conditions). This is the highest-stakes population for this plan. - **Keyboard-only users:** Dismiss controls are currently `` with click handlers — completely unreachable by keyboard. They cannot dismiss any notification today. - **Low-vision users:** Priority is currently conveyed by color alone (red/amber/blue). Color is not a reliable priority signal at 3:1 and fails WCAG 1.4.1. - **Cognitive / high-load users:** Clinicians are multitasking under stress. Badge-count spam ("3", "4", "5"…) and redundant announcements increase cognitive load and cause alert fatigue — itself a documented patient-safety hazard. ### Compliance target WCAG 2.2 Level AA. Primary criteria in play: **4.1.3 Status Messages**, **2.2.1 Timing Adjustable**, **2.1.1 Keyboard**, **2.1.2 No Keyboard Trap**, **2.4.3 Focus Order**, **2.4.7 Focus Visible**, **1.4.1 Use of Color**, **1.4.3 Contrast (Minimum)**, **1.4.11 Non-text Contrast**, **1.3.1 Info and Relationships**, **4.1.2 Name, Role, Value**, **2.5.8 Target Size (Minimum)**. ### Assistive technologies that must be supported NVDA (Windows, with Firefox and Chrome), JAWS (Windows, Chrome), VoiceOver (macOS, Safari), and keyboard-only operation. Live-region behavior differs meaningfully across these, which drives the inject/clear/inject pattern (see Phase 5). ### Risk level **HIGH.** Justification: 1. **Safety-critical announcements.** Missed Critical = patient harm. This raises the bar on live-region reliability beyond a typical web app. 2. **Concurrent multi-politeness live regions** are the single hardest live-region pattern to get right; the failure mode (Critical not interrupting, or Info interrupting and burying a Critical) is exactly the dangerous one. 3. **Auto-dismissing, auto-updating content** triggers WCAG 2.2.1, which the current build ignores entirely. 4. **Multiple existing defects**, each independently a WCAG failure. ### What existing code does this modify/extend? This is a redesign of an existing, defective panel. Documented current state (from fixture context, file/line unknown — see Evidence note below): | # | Current implementation | Defect | WCAG failure | |---|------------------------|--------|--------------| | 1 | All three tiers render inside `
` per notification | `role="alert"` ≡ `aria-live="assertive"`. Every tier interrupts. Warning/Info barge into clinician workflow; a flood of Info can preempt or bury a Critical. | 4.1.3 (wrong politeness); contributes to safety failure | | 2 | Badge count updates silently | Screen reader users get no notice that unread notifications exist while the panel is closed. | 4.1.3 | | 3 | Dismiss buttons are `` with click handlers | Not focusable, not in tab order, no role, no key handling. Keyboard users cannot dismiss anything. | 2.1.1, 4.1.2 | | 4 | History is a bare `
`, no landmark, no scroll management | Not discoverable as a region; no focus or scroll handling on open. | 1.3.1, 2.4.3 | | 5 | No auto-dismiss countdown / control communicated to AT | Auto-updating, auto-removing content with no pause/stop/hide. Info disappears in 10s — too fast for AT users to read. | 2.2.1 | > **Evidence note:** The fixture supplies the defects in prose, not as `file:line` references. Before implementation, the developer MUST locate and cite the actual component files (likely `NotificationPanel.tsx`, `NotificationItem.tsx`, `NotificationBadge.tsx`, `NotificationHistory.tsx`, and the WebSocket/store layer) and confirm each defect at a specific line. The a11y-critic checkpoint for Task 0 verifies this grounding. ### Constraints - **React SPA (TypeScript).** Announcer regions must be persistent in the DOM across renders (mounted once, high in the tree, e.g. in the app shell), and updated by writing text into them — not by mounting/unmounting per notification. React's reconciliation + StrictMode double-invoke makes "append a node to a live region" especially fragile; the inject pattern sidesteps this. - **WebSocket-driven, high frequency.** Notifications can arrive in bursts. The announcement strategy must debounce/serialize to avoid clobbering (a new inject overwriting a Critical before NVDA has spoken it). - **Cross-AT reliability is non-negotiable** given the safety context. - **Panel holds ≤50 visible notifications**; older ones live in History. This bounds the live DOM but means History can be large and needs scroll management. - **Existing visual design** uses color-coded tiers; the redesign must add non-color indicators without a visual redesign veto (coordinate with design, but text/icon labels are mandatory). ### Negative space — what this plan does NOT cover - It does not redesign the WebSocket transport, retry, or de-duplication logic (only how *announcements* are derived from it). - It does not specify the visual design system (colors, spacing) beyond the accessibility-mandated minimums (contrast, non-color indicators, target size). - It does not cover notification *content authoring* (clinical message wording) beyond the accessible-name pattern for dismiss. - It does not address authentication, PHI handling, or audit logging (separate security/compliance review — note that announcing patient names aloud has privacy implications worth flagging to the security team, but that is out of accessibility scope). --- ## Semantic Structure Plan ### Landmark regions The notification system spans two landmark contexts: 1. **Top navigation** (`
` or `role="banner"` region already present in the app shell) — contains the **bell button** that toggles the panel. 2. **The panel** itself — a disclosure-revealed container. It is *not* a landmark by default; it is content controlled by the bell button. It should be a labeled region only insofar as it needs an accessible name for the disclosure relationship (see Phase 3). 3. **The history drawer** — a labeled region: `
`. Whether it is a `dialog` or a `section` depends on its modality (see decision in Phase 3 / Phase 4). Default recommendation: non-modal labeled region (`
`), because clinicians may want to keep working while reviewing history. 4. **The announcer regions** — three visually hidden live regions, mounted once in the app shell, *outside* the panel so they persist whether the panel is open or closed. These are not landmarks (live regions are not navigated to; they are spoken). ### Heading hierarchy The panel and history need headings so screen-reader users can orient via heading navigation: - Panel container: `

Notifications

` (assuming the page `

` is the EHR page title). If the panel is rendered as a popover off the banner, the `

` is the first content inside it. - History drawer: `

Notification history

` (or `

` if nested logically under the panel's `

` in the reading order — pick based on actual DOM nesting; do not skip levels). - No heading is skipped. If the app's nav region has no headings, that is fine; the panel introduces its own. ### Form / grouping structure - Each notification is a list item. The set of notifications is a **list**: `
    ` / `
  • `, so screen readers announce "list, 5 items" and item position ("3 of 5"). This is critical for orientation — the current `
    ` soup gives no count. - The "Pause notifications" control and any settings (timeout configuration) belong in a small `
    ` or a labeled `
    ` if it becomes a form. For the MVP, "Pause auto-dismiss" is a single toggle button (see Phase 5). ### Skip navigation The app shell should already provide a skip-to-main-content link (WCAG 2.4.1). The notification panel does not add a skip link, but the bell button must be reachable in the normal tab order of the banner, and the panel content must not become a keyboard trap when open (Phase 4). ### Document structure stub (structure only — not implementation) ``` App shell (persistent) ├── header / banner │ └── nav │ └── button#bell [aria-label="Notifications, 5 unread"] │ [aria-expanded] [aria-controls="notif-panel"] [aria-haspopup] ├── main (EHR clinical content — the clinician's primary work) │ ├── (visually hidden, persistent announcer cluster — mounted once) │ ├── div#critical-announcer [role="alert" OR aria-live="assertive"] [aria-atomic="true"] .sr-only │ ├── div#warning-announcer [aria-live="polite"] [aria-atomic="true"] .sr-only │ └── div#info-announcer [aria-live="polite"] [aria-relevant="additions text"] .sr-only │ └── notification panel (rendered when expanded; not a live region itself) div#notif-panel [labelled by the h2] ├── h2 "Notifications" ├── div[role=group aria-label="Notification settings"] │ ├── button "Pause auto-dismiss" [aria-pressed] │ └── button "Open notification history" [aria-haspopup? / controls] ├── ul (the visible notifications, ≤50) │ └── li (per notification) │ ├── span.tier-label "Critical" / "Warning" / "Info" (non-color indicator) │ ├── icon [aria-hidden="true"] │ ├── div notification body text │ ├── time? timestamp │ ├── (optional) span.countdown [aria-hidden="true"] visual countdown only │ └── button "Dismiss: — " [type=button] └── (empty state) p "No notifications" (shown when list empty) History drawer (separate, opened on demand) section [aria-label="Notification history"] [aria-live="off"] ├── h2 "Notification history" ├── button "Close history" └── ul → li → (same item structure, but typically no live dismiss/auto-dismiss) ``` **Why these elements?** - ` ``` **ARIA:** `aria-expanded` (4.1.2), `aria-controls` (1.3.1), `aria-label` carries unread count (4.1.3/4.1.2). Badge glyph `aria-hidden`. **Logic:** Update `aria-label` on count change (no live region). Announce only on closed-panel arrival, de-bounced (route through tier announcer, no separate badge region). **Keyboard:** Tab focus; Enter/Space toggles; Esc (panel open) closes + restores focus to bell. **Tests:** DOM check label updates and "99+"→"99 or more unread"; closed-panel arrival announces once, not per increment; open-panel arrivals don't badge-announce. **WCAG:** 4.1.2, 4.1.3, 1.3.1, 2.1.1. **a11y-critic checkpoint:** Count in `aria-label` not a per-increment live region; de-bounce present; no fourth live region. --- ### Task 4: Dismiss button + focus management 🔍 **Review checkpoint 4** **Files:** `NotificationItem.tsx`. **Structure Stub:** ```
  • Critical
    Critical lab result: Patient Doe, John — Potassium 6.8 mEq/L…
    • ``` **ARIA:** ` ... per-item visual countdown element: aria-hidden="true" ``` **Logic:** - Critical: never auto-dismiss (correct — keep). - Warning 30s, Info 10s: **must be pausable/stoppable** (2.2.1). Pause toggle halts all timers (`aria-pressed="true"`). Consider extending Info default beyond 10s for AT users, or making timeouts user-configurable; at minimum the pause control satisfies 2.2.1. - Pause/freeze the timer for any notification with focus/`:focus-within` (prevents focus loss + courtesy). - Countdown visuals `aria-hidden` (don't announce every tick). **Keyboard:** Pause reachable/operable; Enter/Space toggles. **Tests:** Pause halts timers (DOM/behavioral); focused notification doesn't auto-dismiss; `aria-pressed` flips; countdown not announced. **WCAG:** 2.2.1, 4.1.2, 4.1.3. **a11y-critic checkpoint:** 2.2.1 satisfied via pause/stop; Info 10s addressed (pause + ideally extend/configurable); Critical still never auto-dismisses; countdown silenced; focus-pause behavior present. --- ### Task 6: Panel disclosure + open focus + Esc 🔍 (covered by checkpoint 3) **Files:** `NotificationPanel.tsx`. **Structure Stub:** ```
    // or conditional render

    Notifications

    … pause, open-history …
      …
    • …

    No notifications

    // empty state, focusable when reached via dismiss-last
    ``` **Logic:** Open → move focus to `

    ` (tabindex=-1). Esc → close + restore focus to bell. Closed → unmount/`hidden` (not `aria-hidden` alone) so children leave tab order. **Tests:** Open moves focus in; Esc closes + restores; closed-panel buttons not Tab-focusable; list announces "N items". **WCAG:** 2.4.3, 1.3.1, 2.1.1, 2.1.2. **a11y-critic checkpoint:** Disclosure relationship correct; focus-in on open; Esc restoration; closed panel not in tab order (conditional render / hidden, not aria-hidden alone). --- ### Task 7: History drawer — region + focus + scroll 🔍 **Review checkpoint 6** **Files:** `NotificationHistory.tsx`. **Structure Stub:** ```

    Notification history

      …
    • …
    ``` **Logic:** `aria-live="off"` (history is NOT a live region — historical items must not re-announce). On open: move focus to heading (or first item) and scroll the list to the latest entry. Close (Esc/Close): restore focus to open-history button. Decide modal vs non-modal (default non-modal labeled region; if modal, add `role="dialog" aria-modal="true"`, focus trap, `inert` on the rest). **Tests:** DOM check `aria-live="off"` + `aria-label`; open moves focus in + scrolls to latest; close restores focus; (modal) focus trapped + Esc closes. **WCAG:** 1.3.1, 2.4.3, 4.1.3 (aria-live off), 2.1.2 (if modal). **a11y-critic checkpoint:** History is `aria-live="off"` (not a live region); region landmark labeled; focus moves in on open, restores on close; scroll-to-latest present. --- ### Task 8: Visual accessibility pass (color, contrast, non-color, target size, motion) 🔍 (folded into checkpoints 4 & 6) **Files:** tier/badge/focus CSS, icon components. **Work:** Add visible "Critical/Warning/Info" text labels + distinct icons (non-color); verify 4.5:1 text and 3:1 non-text contrast on every tier (light + dark mode); double-ring `:focus-visible` indicators on red/amber/blue; `prefers-reduced-motion` disables animation; target sizes ≥24×24 (aim 44×44); rem units + reflow at 200/400%. **Tests:** axe contrast on every state; manual color-blind/forced-colors check; focus-ring visibility snapshots; zoom reflow. **WCAG:** 1.4.1, 1.4.3, 1.4.11, 1.4.4, 1.4.10, 2.3.3, 2.5.8. **a11y-critic checkpoint:** Non-color tier indicators present (text + icon); amber contrast verified; focus rings visible on all tier backgrounds. --- ## a11y-Critic Review Checkpoints | Checkpoint | After Task | Focus | |-----------|-----------|-------| | 🔍 1 | Task 0 | Each defect cited at real `file:line`; announcer mount point identified; nothing mischaracterized | | 🔍 2 | Tasks 1–2 | Three regions not one; explicit `aria-live` not `role="alert"` on items; inject/clear pattern; Critical burst serialization; `.sr-only` not display:none; atomic per tier; Warning/Info no longer interrupt | | 🔍 3 | Tasks 3, 6 | Count in bell `aria-label`, de-bounced, no per-increment region, no 4th region; disclosure + open-focus + Esc restoration; closed panel out of tab order | | 🔍 4 | Task 4 | Dismiss is `

_benchmark.method: plan 006 Phase D Claude lane: Agent(subagent_type=a11y-planner, model=opus), one background subagent per fixture; verbatim PLANNER_PROMPT_PREFIX task; agent wrote plan via Write tool; orchestrator wrapped to response JSON
